calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar May-15-2023 Assigned To Trial Court

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar May-15-2023 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, being assigned by Department 206 for Prove-Up Hearing as to Defendants Gregory Ovalle and Amy Ovalle.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: N Webb; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 2:06:32 p.m. and concluding at 2:32:04 p.m.

Plaintiff Utrecht & Lenvin, LLP. appearing represented by Patrick Connolly, Esq.
Defendant Gregory Ovalle not appearing.
Defendant Amy Ovalle not appearing.

PAUL UTRECHT is sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff.

PAUL UTRECHT is examined by the Court.

Plaintiff Utrecht & Lenvin, LLP submits the following exhibits for the Court's review:

Exhibit 1: Email correspndence.....................ID/EVID
Exhibit 2: Fee Agreement...........................ID/EVID
Exhibit 3: Invoices................................ID/EVID

The Court takes Judicial Notice of the Notice of time and place of trial filed October 27, 2022.

The matter is submitted.

Based on the documents filed, the statement made by Plaintiff and its Counsel, consideration and good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Utrecht & Lenvin, LLP., and against Defendants Gregory Ovalle and Amy Ovalle, as follows:

Damages: $93,346.74
Prejudgment Interest: $17,464.31

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $110,811.05

Proposed judgment signed and filed this date. (D505)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants Gregory Ovalle And Amy Ovalle To Respond To Written Discovery And Produce Documents; And To Deem Matters Admitted
Off Calendar Jan-27-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF ULTRECH & LENVIN, LLP'S Motion To Compel Defendants Gregory Ovalle And Amy Ovalle To Respond To Written Discovery And Produce Documents; And To Deem Matters Admitted.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Granted, no opposition filed. Defendants Amy Ovalle and Gregory Ovalle shall serve verified Code-complaint responses to form interrogatories and requests for production of documents without objections except privilege within 20 days. The genuineness of Ex A attached to the requests for admission and the truth of any matters specified in the requests for admission are deemed admitted. CCP 233.280.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in Word format repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling, and approved as to form, and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem Aaron Minnis at aaron@minnisandsmallets.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Aaron Minnis; Clerk: R. Sahatjian / M. Goodman; Not Reported. =(302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-27-2022 Set For Trial On May-15-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
